channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: For a quarter century, President Vladimir Putin has faced the specter of Russia's shrinking and aging population. In 1999, a year before he came to power, the number of babies born in Russia plunged to its lowest recorded level.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa poised to become a hurricane and drop torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa is expected to strengthen into a hurricane, threatening the northern Caribbean with massive rainfall and life-threatening flooding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Europe fights to avoid being sidelined by Trump’s world-shaping plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — When President Donald Trump took the stage in Egypt to hail the U.S.-brokered ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas, a row of world leaders stood behind him like extras in a political drama. It was a telling image.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: Japan's new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi faces significant foreign policy challenges just days after taking office.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Friday urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russian oil from two companies to the whole sector, and appealed for long-range missiles to hit back at Russia.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reagan ad that infuriated Trump set to run during World Series
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said Friday the anti-tariffs advertisement featuring former President Reagan, which angered President Trump, will air during the World Series before being paused Monday. The ad led to Trump calling off trade negotiations between Canada and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Trump hits Colombian President Petro with sanctions
item.description: The Trump administration leveled sanctions directly on Colombian President Gustavo Petro and his family members, escalating his feud with the South American leader.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Japan's new leader vows to further boost defense spending as regional tensions rise
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, in her first major policy speech on Friday, pledged to further accelerate Japan’s military buildup and spending and complete an early upgrade of the country’s security strategy as tensions rise with China, North Korea and Russia.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghanistan and Pakistan head to Turkey for second round of crisis talks
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghanistan and Pakistan are heading to Turkey for a second round of crisis talks, officials said Friday, after recent fighting between them killed dozens of people and injured hundreds more.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: The Pentagon is sending the world’s largest aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R. Ford, and its carrier air wing to the Caribbean as the Trump administration conducts strikes against boats it says are smuggling illegal drugs in the region.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Men sentenced over London arson attack masterminded by Russia as part of a wider sabotage campaign
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A British judge on Friday sentenced five men for their roles in an arson attack in London that authorities say was masterminded by Russian intelligence as part of a wider campaign of sabotage and disruption. Their sentences ranged from seven to 17 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US deploys Ford carrier strike group to combat narco-terror in Western Hemisphere
item.description: The Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group is being deployed to continue targeting suspected drug smuggling boats in the Caribbean, the Pentagon said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar in vivid hat, long hidden from view, sells for 32 million euros
item.description: A long-hidden Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar has sold for 32 million euros at an auction in Paris.
item.dc:creator: Oleg Cetinic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Turkish court dismisses case challenging main opposition party's congress
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A Turkish court on Friday dismissed a case challenging the legitimacy of the main opposition party’s 2023 internal election, ruling that there was no legal basis to remove the current leadership from office.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tanzania's first female president has defied early hopes that she would ease repression
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — Less than six months after Samia Suluhu Hassan was inaugurated as Tanzania’s first woman president in 2021, the country’s most famous cartoonist depicted her in a military general’s uniform under the title “Dictatoress.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA and EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa is nearly stationary in the central Caribbean, with forecasters warning it could soon strengthen and brush past Jamaica as a powerful hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: EU leaders agree to future Ukraine funds but make little headway on a plan to use Russia's assets
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders on Thursday ordered the bloc’s executive branch to come up with options for meeting Ukraine’s pressing economic and military needs over the next two years, but did not agree to use frozen Russian assets to fill the gap.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistan bans hard-line Islamist party after recent deadly clashes with police
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan on Thursday banned a radical Islamist party behind recent violent protests that left at least one police officer and four civilians dead, the government said.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Russia won't bow to the US
item.description: President Vladimir Putin cautioned that it would deliver an “overwhelming” response to any military strikes deep inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Ksenia Orlova and Maxim Rodionov, NewsNation

item.title: US strikes two more alleged drug-carrying boats, this time in the Pacific Ocean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. military on Wednesday launched its ninth strike against an alleged drug-carrying vessel, killing three people in the eastern Pacific Ocean, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said, expanding the Trump administration’s campaign against drug trafficking in South America.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EU leaders seek role in Gaza at summit focused on Ukraine and Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders are seeking a more active role in Gaza and the occupied West Bank after being sidelined from the U.S.-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: SAM McNEIL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Zelenskyy calls Trump meeting 'positive' though he didn't get Tomahawk missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says his reportedly tense meeting with U.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jane Goodall's cause of death revealed: Report
item.description: Epilepsy was also listed as one of Goodall's conditions, though it's not clear if it contributed to her death.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: A cargo aircraft skids off a Hong Kong runway into the sea, killing 2 airport workers
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cargo aircraft skidded off a Hong Kong runway and collided with a security patrol vehicle before both fell into the sea early Monday, killing the two people in the car, authorities said. The plane's four crew members were unhurt.
item.dc:creator: CHAN HO-HIM and KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests US will buy Argentinian beef to bring down prices
item.description: “We would buy some beef from Argentina,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One during a flight from Florida to Washington.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel resumes ceasefire in Gaza and says aid deliveries will restart Monday
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Gaza's fragile ceasefire faced its first major test Sunday as Israeli forces launched a wave of deadly strikes, saying Hamas militants had killed two soldiers, and an Israeli security official said the transfer of aid into the territory was halted.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China accuses US of cyberattack on national time center
item.description: China said any damage to related facilities could have disrupted network communications, financial systems and power supply.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Ukraine's Donbas region will have to be 'cut up' to end the Russian invasion
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said Sunday that the Donbas region of Ukraine should be “cut up," leaving most of it in Russian hands, to end a war that has dragged on for nearly four years. “Let it be cut the way it is,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukrainians disappointed after Trump-Zelenskyy meeting fails to immediately secure Tomahawk missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainians shared their disappointment Saturday that the U.S. may not provide Kyiv with long-range Tomahawk missiles, while work to repair the damaged power supply to the country's Zaporizhzhia power plant soothed other concerns surrounding Europe's largest nuclear plant.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
